blocks.content: Thursday evening. One week after. I was home doing homework when Jonathan chirped.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Right. Look. There's something happening Friday after school — Marcus knows a spot, some people going to hang. Maya's gonna be there. I told her you might come.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You told her that without asking me.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Because I knew what you'd say if I asked first.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Bruh I'm trying to help you. You're not going to do it on your own — we both know that. I got you up to speed with Crystal and you waited too long and she's in Atlanta now. I'm not letting that happen again.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You kept pushing me with Crystal. You coached me through every step of that. And she's still gone.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's not — I was trying to help —

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I know you were trying to help. But you decided what help meant without asking me. You kept telling me when to move and how to move and I didn't say anything because you were right that I needed a push. But J —

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I need you to let me make my own mistakes.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm not asking you to stop being my friend. I'm asking you to let me be a person.
